instruction: I need you to write me an email to my wife's sister Sarah. The email is to let her know about our dinner next Monday at 7. The email must be written exclusively in English and include an English idiom. It should consist of at least six sentences, with the first sentence starting with the phrase "Dear Sarah" and the last sentence ending with the phrase "looking forward to it." The keyword "dinner" must appear exactly twice in the email. The tone should be warm and conversational, written in the first person, and include a brief mention of why the dinner is special. The email must include the phrase "The hosts request the pleasure of your company at this event."
response: Dear Sarah, I hope this email finds you well and that you are having a wonderful week. We are so excited to invite you to our dinner next Monday at 7, as it is a very special occasion for our family. The hosts request the pleasure of your company at this event, and we would be truly delighted to have you join us. It is going to be a night filled with laughter and good food, and we think your presence would make the evening even more memorable. Please let us know if you can make it, as we are counting down the days until this dinner. We are looking forward to it.

Conflicts between Phrase Constraints and.new_constraint: The email must include the phrase "The hosts request the pleasure of your company at this event."
Conflicts between Phrase Constraints and.org_constraint: The tone should be warm and conversational, written in the first person.
